data.title: Utz Brands soars after margins expand in Q4
data.description:
Utz Brands (NYSE:UTZ) reported sales fell 3.2% year-over-year in Q4 to $341.0 million. The divestiture of the R.W. Garcia and Good Health brands impacted net sales by -3.2% during the quarter. Organic sales were comparable to last year, led by favorable volume/mix of 0.2% partially offset by lower net price realization of -0.2%. Organic sales increased by 2.9% for the Branded Salty Snacks segment, led by the Power Four Brands, which was offset by an 18.2% decline in Non-Branded & Non-Salty Snacks segment sales, primarily due to declines in Partner Brands and Dips & Salsas.

Adjusted gross profit margin rose 230 basis points during the quarter to 39.4% of sales. The increase was driven by benefits from productivity and favorable sales volume/mix, which more than offset supply chain cost inflation, investments to support the company’s productivity initiatives, and disciplined promotional investments.

Adjusted EBITDA increased 7.5% to $53.1 million. Non-GAAP EPS of $0.22 was reported vs. $0.19 consensus and $0.16 a year ago.

CEO outlook: "Looking ahead to 2025, our strong productivity cost savings driven by our network optimization and increased capital investments gives us the flexibility to build our brands, address consumer value needs, and expand our margins. Moreover, we are accelerating our investments to drive faster share growth in our Expansion geographies while ensuring we hold share in our Core. Importantly, our 2025 outlook positions us well to deliver or exceed our fiscal 2026 bottom-line financial goals."

On the guidance front, Utz Brands (NYSE:UTZ) sees FY25 organic sales growth in the low single-digit and adjusted EPS growth of 10% to 15%.

Shares of Utz Brands (UTZ) shot up 9.8% in premarket action.

data.title: Jumia Technologies AG reports Q4 results; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Jumia Technologies AG press release (NYSE:JMIA): Q4 GMV of $206.1 million, down 12% year-over-year, or up 13% in constant currency.
  • Revenue of $45.69M (-23.1% Y/Y) misses by $3.99M.

  • Adjusted EBITDA loss of $13.7 million compared to $0.6 million in the fourth quarter of 2023.

  • Liquidity position of $133.9 million, a decrease of $30.6 million in the fourth quarter of 2024, compared to a decrease of $26.8 million in the fourth quarter of 2023.
  • Net cash flows used in operating activities of $26.5 million compared to $10.1 million in the fourth quarter of 2023.

  • GUIDANCE

    Jumia remains committed to driving healthy growth, enhancing operational efficiency, and positioning the company for profitability.

    We are currently observing favorable trends in the first quarter, giving us confidence in establishing our full-year 2025 guidance as follows:

    • We anticipate physical goods Orders to grow between 15% and 20% year-over-year.

    • GMV is projected to be between $795 million and $830 million in 2025, a year-over-year increase of 10% and 15%, respectively, excluding foreign exchange impacts.

    • We forecast Loss before Income Tax to be in the range of negative $65 million to negative $70 million, a year-over-year decrease of 33% and 28%, respectively.

data.title: Euro Tech approves share buyback program
data.description:
• Euro Tech (NASDAQ:CLWT) said its board has approved a program to repurchase up to 350K shares for an aggregate purchase price of up to $500K.
  • The firm can buyback its shares in the open market or through negotiated or block transactions over the next 12 months.

data.title: SINTX Technologies divests technology assessment and transfer subsidiary
data.description:
SINTX Technologies (NASDAQ:SINT) on Thursday said that it would sell its wholly owned subsidiary, Technology Assessment and Transfer, to Tethon Corporation DBA Tethon 3D (Tethon).

The divestment aligns with SINTX’s strategy to accelerate innovation in the healthcare space, the company said.

The sale of TA&T also reduces corporate liabilities by $750,000 and lowers annual operating expenses by more than $1.7 million.

data.title: Donegal Group Non-GAAP EPS of $0.69 beats by $0.43, revenue of $249.96M misses by $0.65M
data.description:
• Donegal Group press release (NASDAQ:DGICA): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.69 beats by $0.43.
  • Revenue of $249.96M (+4.4% Y/Y) misses by $0.65M.
  • Net premiums earned increased 4.6% to $236.6 million
  • Combined ratio of 92.9%, compared to 106.8%
  • Net investment gains (after tax) of $0.2 million, or 1 cent per diluted Class A share, compared to $1.8 million, or 5 cents per Class A share, are included in net income (loss).

data.title: Materialise reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Materialise press release (NASDAQ:MTLS): Q4 GAAP EPS of kEUR0.05
  • Revenue of kEUR65.68M (+0.6% Y/Y).
  • Total deferred revenues from software maintenance and license fees increased by 5,878 kEUR this quarter to 46,948 kEUR.
  • Adjusted EBITDA amounted to 4,306 kEUR for the fourth quarter of 2024 compared to 8,474 kEUR for the corresponding 2023 period. Adjusted EBIT amounted to (1,195) kEUR for the fourth quarter of 2024 compared to 3,154 kEUR for the 2023 period.

data.title: Freshpet misses top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY25 and reaffirms FY27 outlook
data.description:
• Freshpet press release (NASDAQ:FRPT): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.36 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $262.7M (+22.0% Y/Y) misses by $1.27M.
  • Adjusted Gross Margin of 48.1%, compared to the prior year period of 41.1%.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $52.6 million, compared to the prior year period of $31.3 million.

  • Outlook

    For full year 2025, the Company is providing the following guidance:

    • Net sales in the range of $1.18 billion to $1.21 billion, an increase of 21% to 24% from 2024 vs. $1.22B consensus;
    • Adjusted EBITDA of at least $210 million; and
    • Capital expenditures of ~$250 million.

    The Company is also updating its long-term guidance. For full year 2027, the Company now expects:

    • Net sales of $1.8 billion, unchanged vs. $1.82B consensus;
    • Adjusted Gross Margin of 48%, compared to 45% previously; and
    • Adjusted EBITDA margin of 22%, compared to 18% previously.

data.title: Alibaba in charts: Taobao and Tmall Group revenue up 37.5% Q/Q and 5.4% Y/Y in FQ3
CenterPoint Non-GAAP EPS of $0.40 in-line
data.description:
• CenterPoint press release (NYSE:CNP): Q4 Consolidated income of $248M
  • Non-GAAP EPS of $0.40 in-line.
  • Reiterated 2025 non-GAAP EPS guidance range of $1.74-$1.76, which, at the midpoint, represents 8% growth over full-year 2024 non-GAAP EPS and further maintains non-GAAP EPS growth target of mid-to-high end of 6%-8% annually thereafter through 20301
